---
title: "VA Stroke Alert"
---

VA STROKE ALERTS with concern for large vessel occlusion
Evaluate patient emergently and obtain noncontrast head CT as you would normally. Do not give contrast (i.e.
CTA) at VA if concern for large vessel occlusion, as availability at the VA will introduce a time delay. You can
give tPA at the VA after discussion with attending if indicated.
If concern for large vessel stroke that may be amenable to endovascular intervention, bring this to the attention of
VA attending immediately. If the VA attending agrees to transfer to UF, he/she must call the Shands transfer
center (352-265-0559) to speak with the on-call stroke attending - do NOT bypass this step by calling the
stroke attending directly. Once the patient has been accepted for transfer, follow the steps below.* If patient is
within tPA window do not delay tPA administration, you can give bolus and start infusion while setting up the
transfer.** Make sure nurse continues to monitor blood pressure with tPA infusion.
To transfer to UF:
Regular Hours (8-4:30p, M-F)
1. Call VA transfer coordinator (Teresa Taylor @ 352-264-3848 or 352-339-9126 or Sharonda Taylor-
Martin @ 386-487-1750 ext 1788) and inform them of need for emergent transfer
2. Place a Non-VA Care (NVCC) Consult (CPRSConsults (order menu)Non VA Care
3. Complete BENEFICIARY TRAVEL (BT) note template (Notes section of CPRS). After completing
the template, an order menu will open, select “GNV Travel IP” option and complete the travel consult
order.
4. Obtain consent using iMed Consent.
5. Enter discharge order (In the Discharge Order dialogue box, next to “Discharge Patient” type in
“Hospital to Hospital Transfer to Shands pending bed availability.”
6. Write a consult note/DC summary
Afterhours/ weekends/holidays
AOD will act as the VA Transfer Coordinator and may be reached at 78-6724 or 78-6004, but otherwise the
process will be the same as during regular hours.
*Make resident on service and/or vascular fellow at UF aware of transfer as well.
**Stay with patient until ambulance arrives to transfer to UF.
Lake City VA telephone requests
Treatment recommendations can only be given for patients whom you have personally evaluated. If you receive a
request for telephone consultation from the Lake City VA, the following are the only two available options:
 If the description sounds like a potential acute stroke within a therapeutic treatment window, you
should advise the physician to contact the VA National Tele-Stroke program from their location or
immediately transfer the patient to the nearest Comprehensive Stroke Center of their choice.
 If the physician calling you describes ANYTHING else, please state the following verbatim: “As a
resident in training, I am not permitted by my Service Chief and Program Directors to provide
consultation over the telephone. If your patient needs urgent Neurological Consultation, I will be
happy to evaluate the patient if he or she is transferred to this hospital or ED. If your patient does not
need urgent Neurological Consultation, please submit a Consult or E-Consult request to LKC or GNV
Neurology.”
